name: Black & White Photography: When to Convert and Why 2026

step.name: Is colour actively fighting the photograph?
step.text: Look for clashing hues, distracting bright elements, colour casts, saturated backgrounds pulling attention from the subject. If colour is genuinely undermining the photograph, conversion solves a real problem. Use carefully — fighting alone isn't enough; strong B&W usually combines this with reason 1.

step.name: Would the photograph still mean what it means without hue?
step.text: Ask what the photograph is about. If meaning lives in tone, light, shape, line, texture, gesture, atmospheric quality, monochrome preserves it. If meaning lives in colour itself — autumn orange, bluebell blue, sunset gold — converting removes the meaning.

step.name: Apply in the field
step.text: At capture: camera mono preview on, compose in monochrome live view, RAW for post flexibility. At review: ask the three questions in order. Yes to Q1 with conviction = convert. Q1 maybe + Q2 yes = convert. Reaching Q3 looking for a reason = leave in colour.
